reasoning: Ten days before the knowledge cutoff (and 13 days before resolution), Polymarket priced the probability of a 25 bps cut at 95%. Without external search tools available to check for major news between October 16 and October 26, this recent market consensus serves as the strongest possible anchor. In general, market expectations for FOMC rate decisions within a couple of weeks of the target meeting are overwhelmingly accurate because the Federal Reserve reliably telegraphs its policy intentions during the pre-meeting period to avoid surprising the markets. Therefore, a 95% probability is maintained as the final estimate.
